back_translation: There was a man from Benjamin, and his name was Kish, son of Abiel, son of Zeror, son of Becorath, son of Aphiah — son of a Benjamite man, a mighty man of valor.
He had a son, and his name was Saul — young and good, and there was no man from the sons of Israel better than he — from his shoulder and upward he was taller than all the people.
The donkeys of Kish father of Saul were lost, and Kish said to Saul his son, 'Take now with you one from the young men, and arise, go, seek the donkeys.'

He passed through the hill country of Ephraim and passed through the land of Shalishah, and they did not find them. They passed through the land of Shaalim, and there was nothing — and he passed through the land of Benjamin, and they did not find.
They came into the land of Zuph, and Saul said to his young man who was with him, 'Come, let us return, lest my father cease from the donkeys and be worried for us.'
He said to him, 'Behold, a man of God is in this city, and a man honored — all that he says surely comes. Now let us go there — perhaps he will tell us our way that we walk in.'
Saul said to his young man, 'But behold, we go — what shall we bring to the man? For the bread is gone from our vessels, and a gift there is not to bring to the man of God. What is with us?'
The young man again answered Saul and said, 'Behold, there is found in my hand a quarter shekel of silver — I will give it to the man of God, and he will tell us our way.'
Formerly in Israel, thus said a man in his going to inquire of God, 'Come and let us go to the seer,' for the prophet of today was formerly called the seer.
Saul said to his young man, 'Good is your word — come, let us go.' And they went to the city where the man of God was.
They were going up in the ascent of the city, and they found young women going out to draw water, and they said to them, 'Is the seer here?'
They answered them and said, 'There is — behold, before you. Hurry now, for today he has come to the city, because the people have a sacrifice today on the high place.'

'As you come to the city, so you will find him before he goes up to the high place to eat — for the people will not eat until his coming, for he blesses the sacrifice; afterward so the called ones eat. And now, go up, for him about this day you will find.'
They went up to the city. They came into the midst of the city — and behold, Samuel was going out to meet them, to go up to the high place.
The LORD had uncovered the ear of Samuel one day before the coming of Saul, saying:

'At this time tomorrow I will send to you a man from the land of Benjamin, and you shall anoint him as ruler over my people Israel, and he shall save my people from the hand of the Philistines — for I have looked upon my people, for their cry has come to me.'
Samuel saw Saul, and the LORD answered him, 'Behold the man whom I spoke to you about — this one shall rule over my people.'
Saul drew near to Samuel in the midst of the gate and said, 'Tell me now where is the house of the seer.'

Samuel answered Saul and said, 'I am the seer. Go up before me to the high place, and you shall eat with me today, and I will send you off in the morning — and all that is in your heart I will tell you.'

'And concerning the donkeys that were lost to you today three days, do not set your heart upon them, for they have been found. And for whom is all the desire of Israel — is it not for you and for all the house of your father?'

Saul answered and said, 'Am I not a Benjamite from the smallest of the tribes of Israel — and my clan is the smallest of all the clans of the tribe of Benjamin? Why have you spoken to me like this word?'
Samuel took Saul and his young man and brought them to the hall, and gave them a place at the head of the called ones — and they were about thirty men.
Samuel said to the cook, 'Give the portion that I gave to you, of which I said to you, Set it with you.'

The cook lifted up the thigh and what was upon it, and set it before Saul, and he said, 'Behold what is left — set before yourself, eat, for to the appointed time it has been kept for you — for I have said, I have called the people.' And Saul ate with Samuel on that day.
They went down from the high place to the city, and he spoke to Saul on the roof.

They rose early, and it was as the dawn was coming up — Samuel called to Saul on the roof, saying, 'Arise, and I will send you off.' Saul arose, and they went out — both of them, he and Samuel, outside.

They were going down at the edge of the city, and Samuel said to Saul, 'Speak to the young man and let him pass on before us — and he passed on — and you stand as today, and I will let you hear the word of God.'
